title: Flow EloTagger
description: Flow EloTagger is a client-side Fabric mod that shows FlowPvP ranks, ELO, and gamemode icons directly in player nametags, tab list, and profile search screens.
body:
#### Flow EloTagger



Flow EloTagger brings FlowPvP ranks directly into Minecraft.



Instead of checking profiles outside the game, you can see player rank, ELO, and gamemode icons while you play. Whether you're fighting, spectating, checking lobbies, or just curious about who you're facing, the information is right there in-game.

[!IMPORTANT]
Disclaimer: This is an independent project and is not an official mod by FlowPvP. It is a community-driven tool developed to enhance the player experience using public data.


## What it does

Flow EloTagger connects to FlowPvP ranking data and displays it in a clean, lightweight, and configurable way.

You can show:

- A selected gamemode rank
- The highest rank automatically
- All available ranks for a player

It also supports gamemode icons, so modes are easier to recognize at a glance.

![example](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7b14d0b62cf76309dc68c9a7b60dddfec5dea05f_0.webp)


## Features

- Rank and ELO displayed above player nametags
- Tab list integration
- Gamemode icons for:
  - Sword
  - Axe
  - UHC
  - Vanilla
  - Mace
  - Diamond Pot
  - Netherite OP
  - SMP
  - Diamond SMP
- Configurable display modes
- In-game player search screen
- Mod Menu config support
- Request caching and rate limiting for better performance

## Why use it?

FlowPvP is competitive, and fast information matters.

Flow EloTagger helps you instantly understand who you're fighting without opening websites or checking stats manually. It is built to make rank information feel natural inside Minecraft, not like a separate tool.

## Requirements

- Minecraft `1.21.11`
- Fabric Loader
- Fabric API
- Mod Menu recommended

## Notes

- This is a **client-side** mod
- It uses FlowPvP API endpoints to fetch ranking data
- Requests are cached to reduce unnecessary API spam and improve responsiveness
